Network is connected to an intermediate node or connection to the server is reset.
Network disconnected. No connection is established, or no network is detected when a socket connection is established.
Call frequency is limited, with up to 5 requests per second.
No available route to the network. Try again once network connection is restored.
The request packet length is greater than the limit, the maximum supported is 1MB.

The request packet has entered the queue to be sent, and part of the data has been sent. A timeout occurs while waiting for the remaining part to be sent. The uplink bandwidth may be insufficient. Please check whether the network is smooth. When calling back the error code, it is detected that there is no Internet connection.
Call failed to due to insufficient buffer resources in the system. System busy. Internal error.

The request packet has entered the network buffer of the system, and the wait for the return packet from the server has timed out. The request packet may not have left the terminal device, the intermediate route has discarded it, the server has accidentally lost the packet, or the return packet has been discarded by the system network layer. There is no error code detected when calling back. networking.
Request packet has entered the network buffer of the system. Timed out waiting for response from server.
Timed out waiting for request packet to enter the to-be-sent queue.
Request packet has entered the to-be-sent queue. Timed out waiting to enter the network buffer of the system.

The request packet length is greater than the limit, the maximum supported is 1MB. The request packet has entered the queue to be sent. The network buffer waiting to enter the system has timed out. There are too many data packets or the sending thread cannot process them. When the error code is called back, it is detected that there is no Internet connection.
The number of Server connections exceeds the limit. Service denied by the server.

The request packet has entered the queue to be sent, and part of the data has been sent. A timeout occurs while waiting for the remaining part to be sent. The uplink bandwidth may be insufficient. Please check whether the network is smooth. When the callback error occurs, it is detected that there is an Internet connection. Internal error
Request queue is full. The number of concurrent requests exceeds the limit of 1000.

Tap here to select an order type. A limit order is an order that will be filled at the preset price; a market order is an order that will be filled at the best available market price immediately.

"· C2C trading is peer-to-peer transaction, buyer pays over-the-counter，seller releases the coins after the payment is made.
· Payment must be done in30 minutes，click“I have paid the order”immediately after payment
· If not, the order is regarded as invalid and cancelled by the system automatically.
· After the order is cancelled, please do not pay again; If you have already paid, do not cancel the order.If you operate incorrectly, please submit the appeal as soon as possible, and upload the proof of payment.
· Please be aware of the risk of C2C trading, and fully understandthe trading rules，and keep you mobile available during the trading.
"

If the copier’s investment is a percentage of that of the lead trader,then the copier may set a multiplier of the same percentage to reduce liquidation risk.
Available here equals to Spot Transferable
You are going to Auto-Copy with %1$s. Are you sure you want to start copying?
"* In this mode, you will fully copy the trader's parameters. Please fully understand the risks involved and exercise caution."

In this mode, you will copy the lead trader’s all futures products available for copying and their respective parameters (leverage, margin mode and trading direction), only with different fund amounts. When the lead trader opens or increases a position,your account will copy with the same percentage of available balance used by the lead trader.When the lead trader reduces or closes a position, your account will copy to close the same percentage of the position. This way, your ROI will be close to that of the lead trader.

"A factor that amplifies the lead trader’s position change. For example, if the multiplier is set as 10, when the lead trader increases their position by 10 contracts, the copier will follow by increasing by 10*10=100 contracts. A lower multiplier can effectively reduce the risk of forced liquidation. Self-Adaptive: Multiplier=Current Total Investment of the Copier/Current Total Assets of the Lead Trader Fixed: A fixed multiplier will be applied to all copy trades."
Stop copying and close your position at the market price automatically when the lead trader you are copying has been inactive for %1$s days.

"Proportional to Available: Copier's Margin=Min{(Lead Trader's Margin/Lead Trader's Current Available Balance)*Copier's Current Available Balance, Limit Per Trade}
Proportional to Total: *Copier's Margin=Min{(Lead Trader's Margin/Lead Trader's Current Total Assets)*Copier's Current Total Investment, Available Balance for Copy Trading, Limit Per Trade}"
"Sharpe Ratio is an important indicator of a lead trader's performance. The higher the Sharpe Ratio, the better the lead trader's risk-adjusted performance (ignoring other trading factors)"
Traders who use Binance API cannot use copy trading on Gate. Please go to Binance to trade

"The higher the IMR, the more assets are available. If the IMR drops to 100% or below, Auto-Cancel will be triggered. IMR = Margin Balance / Initial Margin."

IMR = Margin Balance / Initial Margin. When the IMR (Initial Margin Ratio) is below 100%, the system will auto-cancel your spot open orders to check if the IMR is above 100%; if not, the system will start to cancel open orders of derivatives. In this scenario, you can only submit orders that can reduce account risks.
"The higher the MMR (Maintenance Margin Ratio), the safer the account. If the MMR drops to 100% or below, liquidation will be triggered. MMR = Margin Balance / Maintenance Margin."

MMR = Margin Balance / Maintenance Margin. When the MMR (Maintenance Margin Ratio) drops to 100% or below, liquidation will be triggered. For more details about the calculation of the MMR in the Portfolio Margin Mode, please go to Portfolio Margin Mode - About Margin and Risk Control Mechanism
Margin Balance = ∑(Positive Equity x Index Price x Margin Adjustment Factor) + ∑(Negative Equity x Index Price) - Haircut Loss - Options Value
Margin Balance = ∑(Positive Equity × Index Price × Discount Rate) + ∑(Negative Equity × Index Price) – Haircut Loss – Options Value
Margin Balance = ∑(Positive Equity x Index Price x Margin Adjustment Factor) + ∑(Negative Equity x Index Price)
Margin Balance = ∑(Positive Equity × Index Price × Discount Rate) + ∑(Negative Equity × Index Price) – Haircut Loss
The profit and loss of all positions at the time of closing, calculated by the mark price.
Wallet Assets = Total Net Transfer In + Total Realized PnL + Total Net Funding Fee - Total Trading Fee
A BBO limit order is an order to trade at the best counterparty price in the order book at the time of placement, and any unfilled portion will remain in the order book, waiting to be executed. %1$s
A limit order is an order to trade at a preset price, but it is not guaranteed to be executed.
The order price will auto-update to the best price on the order book.

A chase limit order is a limit order placed at the best bid or ask price. It dynamically adjusts the order price based on market fluctuations until the order is filled, canceled, or reaches the max chase distance. %1$s

Place a limit order at the best bid or ask price on the order book, and the order price will auto-update to the best price until the order is filled, canceled, or reaches the preset max chase distance.

Iceberg Pro is a strategy used to handle large orders by splitting them into a series of smaller orders, which helps to reduce slippage and achieve better fill prices. %1$s

When selling an asset: If the market price of the trading pair falls below this price, the Iceberg Pro strategy will stop placing split sell orders until the market price returns to or exceeds it."

Each order is placed at a fixed distance from the best bid or ask price. When the market price deviates from the order price by a certain distance, the order price will be adjusted to ensure a better execution price.

A trailing stop order is a strategy order that submits a preset order to the market when the price pulls back. The trigger price adjusts with market fluctuations, and once triggered, a market order will be placed.

" Scenario:
Assume you hold a long position, the price is $100, and you set a trailing stop sell order with a $30 loss limit and an activation price of $150.
·If the price rises to $140 and then falls $30 to $110, your trailing stop sell order will not be triggered because it has not been activated.
·If the price rises to $150, your trailing stop sell order will be activated. If the price continues to rise to $200 and then falls $30 to $170, your trailing stop sell order will be triggered and converted into a market sell order. "

The pullback rate is the main condition for calculating the actual trigger price, ensuring that the trigger price can dynamically adjust with the market price. The effective range of the pullback rate is 0.1% to 10%. For example, if you place a sell order with a 5% pullback rate and the underlying price rises to $60,000 before pulling back, your actual trigger price will be $60,000 × (1 – 5%) = $57,000.

The activation price is one of the trigger conditions for a trailing stop order. When the last price/mark price/index price reaches or exceeds the activation price, the order will be activated. Once activated, the system begins calculating the actual trigger price of the trailing stop order. If the activation price is not specified, the order will be activated immediately after placement.

" Scenario:
Assume you hold a long position, the price is $100, and you set a trailing stop sell order with a 10% loss limit.
· If the price falls 10% from $100 to $90, your trailing stop sell order will be triggered and converted into a market sell order.
· If the price rises to $150 and then falls 7% to $140, your trailing stop sell order will not be triggered.
· If the price rises to $200 and then falls 10% to $180, your trailing stop sell order will be triggered and converted into a market sell order. "

" Scenario:
Assume you hold a long position, the price is $100, and you set a trailing stop sell order with a $30 loss limit.
· If the price falls $30 from $100 to $70, your trailing stop sell order will be triggered and converted into a market sell order.
· If the price rises to $150 and then falls $20 to $130, your trailing stop sell order will not be triggered.
· If the price rises to $200 and then falls $30 to $170, your trailing stop sell order will be triggered and converted into a market sell order. "

PreMint is a service that supports users to stake USDT to mint PreTokens and trade them with an ideal price in spot markets.

The current market is a PreToken spot market, where users can trade minted token credentials for tokens that are yet to be issued or listed (“PreToken”). The market will be closed before the token is officially listed and the PreToken balance will be auto-settled, based on the staking and redemption conditions at its corresponding Pre-Market service.

The current contract is a pre-market contract, which users can trade in the pre-market futures market. Please note that the pre-market has high volatility, so operate with caution to avoid unnecessary risks.
Pre-Market OTC is an over-the-counter trading service that allows investors to buy and sell new tokens at expected prices and quantities before the tokens officially get listed.

Time-Weighted Average Price (TWAP) is an algorithmic trading strategy where users can split a large order into multiple smaller orders over a period of time and place them at specified time intervals. %1$s
Traders can set the order price via \"Var.\" or \"Ratio\" based on the liquidity. When the order is activated, the strategy will start to place small orders according to the preset \"Time Interval\".
Split large orders and place orders at regular intervals to reduce slippage.

A recurring buy plan is a simple tool that automates crypto currency investments. It uses %1$s to help you build a diversified portfolio. You simply select the cryptocurrency you want to buy, set the amount, and choose the buying frequency. This plan eliminates the need for manual trades each time.

"1.After the strategy is created, the system will place an initial order to verify your identity. If you don’t select “Place an order immediately after the strategy is created,” the system will place a zero-dollar order. 2.You cannot modify the strategy within 10 minutes of creation. 3.The price for each recurring buy is determined by the real-time market price at the time of each purchase."
Can I cancel my recurring buy plan?
Yes, you can cancel your plan anytime in the “Plan List.”
Can I modify my recurring buy plan?
Yes, you can adjust the parameters anytime in the “Plan List.”
How many cards can I link for recurring buys?
You can link up to 5 credit/debit cards for recurring buys, with support limited to Visa and Mastercard.
We are performing essential upgrades to enhance your service experience. Please check back later!
What payment methods are supported for recurring buys?
We currently support credit/debit card payments for recurring buys, limited to Visa and Mastercard.
How is the price determined for recurring buys?

The buy price is based on the real-time market price at the time of each scheduled purchase. Since the price may vary with each buy, users should monitor the market closely after setting up their plan.
What happens if my purchase fails due to insufficient funds? Will the system automatically try to make the next scheduled purchase?
The system will retry on the next scheduled purchase date. After 3 consecutive failed payments, the plan will automatically terminate.
Once a scheduled purchase is completed, it cannot be refunded. However, you can cancel your recurring buy plan at any time.
The crypto price may vary according to market conditions. Please see the final price on the order confirmation page.

The merchant has transaction requirements. You must meet the following conditions to place an order.
The merchant only deals with users that meet the following conditions. Check if you meet them or not.
How do I complete the payment?
For P2P transactions, you need to complete the payment based on the payment methods and details provided by the seller in the order. For more information, please refer to the Guide in Help Center.
The other party requests personal details for verification

To prevent potential money laundering issues, some sellers may require additional verification to confirm the source of funds. You can complete the verification in the chat window. If you do not wish to proceed with the verification, you may cancel the order and filter for other orders that do not require verification.
The other party asks me to use other contact methods and trade outside the platform

Do not trust transfer requests from strangers online. Always conduct transactions securely through the platform. Verify the authenticity of the other party’s identity. If you suspect suspicious behavior, report it to the platform and cancel the order.
The other party charges me additional fees
Currently, the P2P platform does not charge any fees to users. If a trader requests extra fees, you can report them to the platform and cancel the order.
How long will it take to receive the cryptocurrency I purchased?

Once the seller confirms receipt of your payment, you will receive your cryptocurrency right away. However, the transfer time may vary depending on the payment method. Please check with your payment provider for more details. If you do not receive your cryptocurrency within the specified time, please file a dispute immediately, and our customer service team will assist you in resolving the issue. [Dispute Guidelines]
What should I do if I no longer wish to continue the transaction?

If you’ve already made the payment, do not cancel the order directly. Instead, contact the seller to negotiate a refund and cancel the order, or reach out to customer service for assistance. If you haven’t made the payment yet, you can cancel the order.

It indicates the order of the current position in the auto-deleverage queue. The more lights are lit, the greater the probability that your position will be reduced in the case of ADL events.

Tips: The current position will be closed at market price and a same-size position on the opposite side will be opened. If available funds are insufficient, the position will be opened with the maximum allowable amount. Please note that due to factors such as margin requirements and market conditions, it cannot be guaranteed that the position will be reversed 100% successfully.

BBO (Best Bid and Offer) refers to the best bid and ask price. When placing an order, the BBO order price automatically adjusts to the highest bid or lowest ask price in the order book. If the order book has limited supply, the unfilled portion of the order will remain at that price, waiting to be executed.
BBO is unavailable when the TP/SL is checked
When using BBO, the system auto-adjusts the order price to the corresponding order book level with the highest price precision and places a limit order. %1$s

It is the mechanics used to tether to spot price, applied every 8 hours. Funding payment is based on the position value a user holds at the applying timestamp. If funding rate is positive, longs pay shorts; if negative, shorts pay longs.

"It is the mechanics used to tether to spot price, applied every %1$s hours. Funding payment is based on the position value a user holds at the funding timestamp. If funding rate is positive, longs pay shorts; if negative, shorts pay longs. Funding = Position Value x Funding Rate"
"Orders placed in unselected pairs won't be copied, but their PnL still affects your lead trading data and may trigger a %s."
The two values displayed represent the current drawdown and drawdown that triggers the forced stop loss respectively.

The risk control system uses the internationally recognized modified Dietz method to calculate NAVPS (Net Asset Value per Share). The forced stop-loss drawdown ratio is a configurable parameter. The current drawdown is calculated based on the highest and current NAVPS within the risk control period (the past 30 calendar days), using the following formula:
Current Drawdown = (Highest NAVPS − Current NAVPS) / Highest NAVPS.
The maximum leverage you can use when placing orders in the current market.
Real Leverage = Total Value of Positions Held and To be Increased / Total Assets. Note: The data below indicate current real leverage and maximum real leverage of the futures account respectively.
Unused Position Quota = Total Assets * Real Leverage Upper Limit - Total Value of Positions Held and To be Increased

If the number of forced stop-losses reaches the set limit during the current risk control period, your account will enter a cooling-off period, during which trading or deposits/withdrawals are prohibited. Displayed values: risk control period time / number of forced stop-losses / maximum allowed forced stop-losses
Risk control parameters configured for lead traders,

After calculating the account net value based on the Net Asset Value Method, the forced stop-loss ratio is derived from the highest net value and the current net value within the current risk control period. The data below indicate current drawdown, and drawdown that triggers the forced stop loss respectively.

Estimated deviation between liquidation price and mark price

The deviation ratio between liquidation price and mark price reflects the risk of the position being liquidated after the current order is filled. The closer the deviation ratio is to 0, the higher the liquidation risk. On the contrary, the further the deviation ratio is to 0, the lower the liquidation risk.
Mark price is used to determine the position value, liquidation condition and funding rate. It is calculated by the spot index price and the premium index.

Maintenance margin ratio (MMR) is used to assess the risk status of a contract position. The lower the MMR, the higher the position risk. Liquidation will be triggered when the MMR falls to 100% or below.

Maintenance Margin Ratio (MMR) is used to assess the risk status of a contract position. The lower the MMR, the higher the position risk. Liquidation will be triggered when the MMR falls to 100% or below.
Maintenance Margin Ratio = Margin Balance / Maintenance Margin; when the MMR is ≤ 110%, loan will be auto-repaid using available balance; when the MMR is ≤ 100%, liquidation will be triggered.

After creation, launch platform tokens will be traded internally to gain liquidity before migrating to an external exchange. Listing conditions on different platforms vary. Tokens are untradeable during migration until listing succeeds.

The current market cap = Price x Circulating Supply. As the price here may differ from the onchain price, the current market cap is for reference only.
Increase in open interest + increase in trading volume: Funds are entering the market, indicating strong momentum in the current trend, which may continue.

Increase in open interest + decrease in trading volume: Funds flow is slowing down, but with an increase in open interest, it may suggest that market participants are starting to observe or adjust strategies.
Decrease in open interest + increase in trading volume: This could indicate positions being closed, with funds flowing out of the market, signaling a potential trend reversal or consolidation phase.
Decrease in open interest + decrease in trading volume: The market may lack directional momentum, resulting in low volatility or a wait-and-see state.

The delivery and settlement results can be predicted based on the current PreToken price in the PreToken spot market, which are for reference only. Please note that the estimated settlement result could vary from the actual result and the actual result shall prevail.

To ensure users can earn returns at settlement, the transaction price in the current PreToken spot market has to be no greater than the settlement risk price, which is calculated based on the total staked and minted amount. When purchasing Pretokens at a price higher than this price, you are not likely to receive adequate compensation when minters default on delivery. Note: The settlement risk price can change dynamically according to the staking conditions.

"Total delivered amount and remaining staked assets will be allocated proportionally to participants based on their balance on the snapshots. Token Received = (Balance on the Snapshot / Total Minted) * Total Delivered Staked Assets Received = (Balance on the Snapshot / Total Minted) * Remaining Staked Assets"

"Support Line: A horizontal line below which bullish pressure may appear when the price falls, acting as a stop-loss level.
Resistance Line: A horizontal line at which the price may no longer rise and tends to fall instead, acting as a take-profit level.
SRL: An AI-powered indicator that generates support and resistance lines based on the BOLL20 line."

The Support and Resistance Indicator is an AI-powered market analysis tool based on the BOLL20 line, designed to help identify potential support and resistance areas. This indicator combines market data with intelligent algorithms to dynamically generate up to five support lines and five resistance lines, providing an intuitive analysis of price trends.

"VOL (Volume) is one of the key indicators of market activity. It represents the total trading volume over a specific period. "

"Assessing Trend Strength Rising with high volume: Price rises with increasing volume, indicating strong buying power and a potentially sustained trend. Rising with low volume: Price rises while volume decreases, suggesting weakened buying power and a possible pullback. Falling with high volume: Price falls with increasing volume, indicating strong selling pressure and a potential acceleration of the downtrend. Falling with low volume: Price falls while volume decreases, suggesting weakening selling pressure and possible stabilization or a rebound. "

"Identifying Volume Divergence Signals New high with low volume: When the price hits a new high but volume shows no significant increase, it may indicate weakening buying power and a potential pullback. New low with low volume: When the price hits a new low and volume remains low, it may indicate reduced selling pressure and a potential rebound. "

"Combining with Price Patterns Volume during breakouts: A substantial increase in volume during a breakout strengthens the reliability of the price movement when it breaches key support or resistance levels. Fake breakout detection: If the price breaks support or resistance levels with low volume, it may indicate a false breakout or a bull/bear trap. "

The current market is a PreToken spot market, where users can trade minted token credentials for tokens that are yet to be issued or listed (“PreToken”). The market will be closed before the token is officially listed and the PreToken balance will be auto-settled, based on the staking and redemption conditions at its corresponding Pre-Market service.
Pre-Market OTC is an over-the-counter trading service that allows investors to buy and sell new tokens at expected prices and quantities before the tokens officially get listed.

"Alpha specifically targets emerging Digital Assets, which are generally expected to have lower market capitalization and unique or novel characteristics. A Digital Asset which has been made available in Alpha does not directly or indirectly imply in any way that the same Digital Asset will be listed in the spot and futures market of Gate Exchange in the future.
As the prices of Digital Assets may fluctuate, the value of any investment you make in Digital Assets in Alpha may increase or decrease accordingly, and it is possible to lose the entire amount or part of the amount you invested. You enter into transactions with full awareness of the possibility of rapid and substantial asset value changes. Please note that: (i) Due to the significant price volatility of Digital Assets in Alpha, the risk of losing all or part of your investment will be magnified; (ii) By purchasing Digital Assets in Alpha, you will be exposed to price slippage and blockchain fees, which may negatively affect the price of Digital Assets in Alpha; and (iii) Digital Assets in Alpha cannot be withdrawn from the Gate Exchange, you can only deposit and withdraw Digital Assets in Alpha from your Alpha account.
You are solely responsible for your investment decisions and you acknowledge all investments in Digital Assets carry the risk of loss. Under no circumstances will Gate be responsible or liable for any losses you may suffer, directly or indirectly, as a result of investing in Digital Assets in Alpha."

2. 10 gold coins will be rewarded for your first like or comment every day.
3. If on any day during the event, you have redeemed gifts and earned less than 100 gold coins, you will have a chance to double your gold coins earned that day.
4. Likes and comments are counted, regardless of whether they are deleted or canceled afterwards.
5. No matter how many comments you make on the same post, only one entry will be counted and rewarded.
6. Liking, commenting or replying your own posts can't qualify for gold coins.
7. Replying to comments can't qualify for gold coins.
8. Only posts posted on the day count for the event.
9. If a post contains content that induces others to like or comment, the post will not be qualified as a hot post and all gold coins obtained by liking/commenting on this post will be canceled.
10. In the first round of the event, a total of 5000 packages are offered on a first come, first served basis, each with 700 gold coins (redeemable for 10 Points)."

Bollinger Bands consist of a band of three lines which are plotted in relation to security prices. The line in the middle is usually a Simple Moving Average (SMA). The SMA then serves as a base for the Upper and Lower Bands. The Upper and Lower Bands are used as a way to measure volatility by observing the relationship between the Bands and price. Typically the Upper and Lower Bands are set to two standard deviations away from the SMA (The Middle Line) ; however the number of standard deviations can also be adjusted by the trader.

The Double Exponential Moving Average (EMA) is a technical indicator that uses two moving averages to help confirm uptrends when price moves above average, and confirm downtrends when the price moves below average. A trend change might be occurring when the price moves in a direction away from the average. In addition, moving averages can be used to signify support and/or resistance territories.

KDJ is designed to display the location of the close compared to the high/low range over a user defined number of periods. Typically, it is used for three things; Identifying overbought and oversold levels, spotting divergences and also identifying bull and bear set ups or signals.

Moving Average (MA) is a price based, lagging (or reactive) indicator that displays the average price of a security over a set period of time. A Moving Average is a good way to gauge momentum as well as to confirm trends, and define areas of support and resistance.

MACD employs two Moving Averages of varying lengths (which are lagging indicators) to identify trend direction and duration. Then, MACD takes the difference in values between those two Moving Averages (MACD Line) and an EMA of those Moving Averages (Signal Line) and plots that difference between the two lines as a histogram which oscillates above and below a center Zero Line. The histogram is used as a good indication of a security momentum.

The On Balance Volume indicator (OBV) is used to measure the relationship between changes in trading volume and price action. OBV is primarily used to identify the current price trends or to anticipate potential price reversal. For example, if OBV increases as price rises, buying pressure is increasing and the trend may continue; If OBV decreases as price falls, selling pressure is increasing and may signal a price reversal.

The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is a well versed momentum based oscillator which is used to measure the speed (velocity) as well as the change (magnitude) of directional price movements. Essentially RSI, when graphed, provides a visual mean to monitor both the current, as well as historical, strength and weakness of a particular market. The strength or weakness is based on closing prices over the duration of a specified trading period creating a reliable metric of price and momentum changes. Given the popularity of cash settled instruments (stock indexes) and leveraged financial products (the entire field of derivatives); RSI has proven to be a viable indicator of price movements.

SAR (Parabolic SAR) is primarily used to signal the price trend and identify points of potential stops and reverses. The SAR in Parabolic SAR stands for “Stop and Reverse”. This indicator helps traders to set stop-loss conditions as well as entry and exit time. The indicator’s calculations create a parabola consisting of a series of points that lie below price in a bullish trend and above price in a bearish trend. Once the price breaks these points, the trend may reverse.

The Stoch RSI (Stochastic RSI indicator) is a technical analysis indicator that combines Stochastic Oscillator and Relative Strength Index (RSI). It is primarily used for identifying overbought and oversold conditions. When the Stoch RSI approaches 1, the assets may be overbought; when it approaches 0, the assets may be oversold.

Williams %R (%R) is a momentum-based oscillator used in technical analysis, primarily to identify overbought and oversold conditions. The %R is based on a comparison between the current close and the highest high for a user defined look back period. %R Oscillates between 0 and -100 (note the negative values) with readings closer to zero indicating more overbought conditions and readings closer to -100 indicating oversold.

Interest is deducted automatically every hour on the hour after you borrowed successfully. Lending offers in the market will be sorted based on their minimum interest rates to find out the single execution interest rate for the past hour, or the hourly lending rate, which will be used to calculate the borrowing rate. Daily Borrowing Rate (Floating) = Hourly Lending Rate * 24 + (Hourly Lending Rate * 24 * Service Fee Rate).

A market order will immediately be filled at the best available price from the order book. The final execution price may be better or worse than expected due to price fluctuations.

A market order is an order to buy or sell immediately at the available counterparty price in the market, suitable for quick market entry purpose. However, slippage losses could occur for large orders.

You can preset the trigger price and order price for SL/TP orders. When the latest price reaches the trigger price for either take-profit or stop-loss, the corresponding order will be triggered and placed at the preset order price, and the other side will be automatically canceled.

"Scenario: Assume you hold 1 BTC with the latest price at 92,000. You set a %1$s order to sell 1 BTC at market price with a %2$s of 100,000 and a %3$s of 80,000 respectively. ·If the price ≥ 100,000, the %4$s will be triggered, and 1 BTC will be sold at market price. ·If the price ≤ 80,000, the %5$s will be triggered, and 1 BTC will be sold at market price."
When order of either side is triggered, the other side will be auto canceled.

Traders set the order price based on liquidity using the \"Var.\" and \"Ratio\". Taking buying as an example, when a strategy starts running, a small buy order is placed at the price with a variance to \"sell 1\" by a certain var./ratio. If not set, a default slippage rate will be applied in setting the price .
The percentage value should be between 0% and 100%.

The delivery and settlement results can be predicted based on the current PreToken price in the PreToken spot market, which are for reference only. Please note that the estimated settlement result could vary from the actual result and the actual result shall prevail.

The settlement risk price is calculated based on the total staked and minted amount. When purchasing Pretokens at a price higher than this price, you are not likely to receive adequate compensation when minters default on delivery. Therefore, please make sure that your transaction price is lower than the settlement risk price.

Minters need to stake USDT to mint PreTokens for Pre-Market trading. During the delivery period, minters must deposit or buy real tokens for delivery to get their staked assets back. Otherwise, the system will forfeit the staked assets. After the real token is listed in spot markets, PreToken holders will receive real tokens delivered, along with forfeited staked assets as compensation if minters default on delivery proportionally.
The total amount of minted PreToken. After the real token is listed in spot markets, system will conduct a settlement based on the delivery outcome.

The total amount of minted token credentials, or Points. After the real token is listed in spot markets, the system will conduct a settlement based on the actual supply of real tokens and the delivery outcome.
The total amount of minted PreToken after conversion, calculated by Total Minted (Points) x Conversion Ratio. The system will conduct a settlement based on the delivery outcome.
Price variance should be greater than 0

Before submitting an order, you can preset TP/SL conditions, which will come into effect after the order is fully filled.After submitting the order, the system will set TP/SL orders based on your actual filled amount and the preset TP/SL conditions will expire when the order is canceled.

You can preset TP/SL for the current order. After the order is fully filled, the system will create TP/SL orders with preset trigger price and order price. Once the trigger price is reached, the TP/SL orders will be activated. The preset TP/SL conditions will become invalid if the current order is canceled.

You can preset TP/SL for the current order. After the order is fully filled, the system will create TP/SL orders with preset trigger price and order price. Once the trigger price is reached, the TP/SL orders will be activated. The preset TP/SL conditions will become invalid if the current order is canceled.

The actual fill price of TP/SL orders may deviate from the preset price due to significant market volatility, and there is a possibility that the TP/SL orders may not be filled or may not be fully filled.

The activation price is the activation conditions for a trailing stop order. When the last price/mark price/index price reaches or exceeds the activation price, the order will be activated. Once activated, the system begins calculating the actual trigger price of the trailing stop order. If the activation price is not specified, the order will be activated immediately after placement.

The activation price is the activation condition for a trailing stop order. When the last price reaches or exceeds the activation price, the order will be activated. Once activated, the system begins calculating the actual trigger price of the trailing stop order. If the activation price is not specified, the order will be activated immediately after placement.
Track the market price, and once the preset value is reached, a market order will be automatically placed.

"Scenario: Assume you hold a long position, with the current market price at $100, and you set a trailing stop sell order with a 10% loss threshold. ·If the price drops by 10% from $100 to $90, your trailing stop sell order will be triggered and converted into a market sell order. ·If the price rises to $150 and then drops by 7% to $140, your trailing sell order will not be triggered. ·If the price rises to $200 and then drops by 10% to $180, your trailing sell order will be triggered and converted into a market sell order."

"Scenario: Assume you hold a long position, with the current market price at $100, and you set a trailing stop sell order with a $30 loss and an activation price of $150. ·If the price rises to $140 and then drops by $30 to $110, the trailing stop sell order will not be triggered because it has not been activated yet. ·If the price rises to $150, the trailing stop sell order will be activated. As the price continues to rise to $200 and then drops by $30 to $170, the trailing stop sell order will be triggered and converted into a market sell order."

A Trailing stop order is a preset strategic order that will be submitted to the market when there is a pullback. Its trigger price can change with market fluctuations. Once it is triggered, a market order will be placed.

Time-weighted average price (TWAP) is an algorithmic trading strategy where users can split a large order into multiple smaller orders over a period of time and place them at specified time intervals.

Interest starts to accrue at the start of the next hour following a successful borrowing transaction and is calculated based on the hourly interest rate and the outstanding loan amount at that time. The hourly rate and the annual percentage rate (APR) fluctuate according to market borrowing demand and are updated hourly.

What best describes the industry in which you work?

"As part of our commitment to maintaining a secure and compliant financial environment, we want to inform you that Gate US. strictly adheres to the regulations set forth in the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) laws. These regulations require financial institutions to implement robust procedures to detect and prevent money laundering, terrorist financing, and other illicit activities. To fulfill our regulatory obligations, we have established comprehensive compliance programs, which include: Customer Identification: We will verify your identity and collect certain information to establish and maintain a customer relationship. Transaction Monitoring: We continuously monitor transactions for suspicious activities and report any potential red flags to the appropriate authorities. Enhanced Due Diligence: In certain cases, we may conduct enhanced due diligence procedures for high-risk customers or transactions to mitigate potential risks. By proceeding with the onboarding process, you acknowledge and agree to cooperate with our efforts to comply with BSA/AML regulations. We may request additional information or documentation from you to fulfill our regulatory obligations."

Gate US, Inc. (Gate) currently provides individual retail accounts for US residents only. As a regulated financial institution, Gate is obligated to follow certain KYC, or Know Your Customer, guidelines, including verifying the identity of its customers. Failure to attest to the following will result in your inability to successfully complete required KYC checks and may prevent you from opening an account.
1. I am a lawful resident of a U.S. state or territory.
2. I am seeking to open an account for my own individual benefit.

Governmental Affiliation: Have you or an immediate family member held a prominent public position—in the past or present—such as a senior government or military official, a judge, political leader, or executive at a state-owned company?
